goal: Profiler L3: cross-column referential integrity discovery and orphan detection in source database

acceptance_criteria.description: SourceRelationship model in models/types.go: relationship_id (UUID), profiling_run_id, parent_table (schema.table), parent_column, child_table (schema.table), child_column, relationship_type ('FK_DECLARED'|'FK_INFERRED'), confidence (float64, 0.0-1.0 — 1.0 for declared FKs, heuristic-scored for inferred), orphan_count (int — child rows with no matching parent), orphan_pct (float64 — orphan_count / total child rows), created_at. Migration 054_source_relationships.sql creates migration.source_relationship table. RLS Tier B via profiling_run — engagement. Index on (profiling_run_id). UNIQUE(profiling_run_id, parent_table, parent_column, child_table, child_column).

L3Executor in worker/profile_l3_executor.go implementing worker.Executor interface (matching existing L1/L2 pattern in worker/profile_l1_executor.go). Phase 1: Discovers declared foreign keys from information_schema.table_constraints + key_column_usage (for PostgreSQL/MySQL) or sys.foreign_keys (for MSSQL). Stores as FK_DECLARED with confidence=1.0. Phase 2: Infers FK relationships by name-matching heuristics: columns ending in '_id' that match another table's PK column name, with confidence scored by: exact name match (0.9), partial name match (0.7), type-compatible only (0.5). Phase 3: Orphan detection — for each discovered relationship (declared and inferred with confidence >= 0.7), runs SELECT COUNT(*) FROM child LEFT JOIN parent ON child.fk = parent.pk WHERE parent.pk IS NULL. Stores orphan_count and orphan_pct.

L3Executor registered in main.go with job_type='profile_l3'. Updates profiling run status to RUNNING_L3 at start, level_reached=3 on completion. Stores results via db.InsertSourceRelationships (batch insert). Progress updates: phase 1/3 (FK discovery), 2/3 (inference), 3/3 (orphan detection) via q.UpdateProgress. WebSocket broadcast: profiling_l3_completed with engagement_id, relationship_count, orphan_relationships (count of relationships with orphan_count > 0).

DB operations in db/profiling.go: InsertSourceRelationships(db, []models.SourceRelationship), ListSourceRelationships(db, profilingRunID, page, perPage) with orphan_count > 0 filter option, GetOrphanSummary(db, profilingRunID) returning aggregate {total_relationships, orphan_relationships, total_orphan_rows, highest_orphan_pct}. GET /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/profiling/{runId}/relationships returns paginated relationships. GET /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/profiling/{runId}/orphan-summary returns aggregate orphan metrics. Supports ?orphans_only=true filter to show only relationships with orphan_count > 0.

critical: Source DB queries are read-only — no writes to source system during profiling
Orphan detection uses LEFT JOIN pattern, not NOT IN (handles NULLs correctly)
FK inference heuristics produce confidence scores — never asserted as fact without human review
L3 requires L2 completion (level_reached >= 2) — profiling run must have source_column data
high: Orphan detection only runs for relationships with confidence >= 0.7 — avoids expensive queries on weak matches
Batch insert for relationships — single transaction for all discoveries in one run
Progress updates per phase (1/3, 2/3, 3/3) — UI can show current L3 stage
Source connection reuses engagement's stored credentials — same pattern as L1/L2 and drift detector
medium: Inference heuristics are driver-aware — PostgreSQL vs MySQL vs MSSQL information_schema differences handled
UNIQUE constraint prevents duplicate relationship records on re-run
orphan_pct computed as orphan_count / total child rows — not parent rows
